title: commandWindows resolves git root via shell-agnostic node -e bootstrap; contract test executes it from nested cwd
action: hooks/catalog.mjs commandWindowsFor and skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs codexHookCommandWindows currently emit cwd-relative node .bee/bin/hooks/<f> --source=repo which breaks from any subdirectory (MODULE_NOT_FOUND). Replace with a shell-agnostic bootstrap: node -e SCRIPT with the hook filename passed as trailing argv (read via process.argv) — SCRIPT uses ONLY single quotes internally and NO dollar, percent, or backtick characters (cmd.exe + PowerShell both parse the outer double-quoted string identically): it runs git rev-parse --show-toplevel via child_process.execSync; on failure exits 0 silently (POSIX parity: no git root = transport unavailable); on success spawnSync of process.execPath with [join(root,'.bee','bin','hooks',hookfile),'--source=repo'] and stdio inherit, then process.exit with the child status (1 on spawn error). hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs: update the NODE_BARE_SOURCE_REPO contract (L1112-1149) to require the new bootstrap form (still forbid dollar/percent/backtick and shell metacharacters); ADD an execution test that actually runs the emitted commandWindows string with cwd set to a NESTED subdirectory of a fixture repo (node -e is cross-platform so this runs on Linux CI) and asserts the real hook executes (root resolved) and that outside a git repo it exits 0. Regenerate the committed .codex/hooks.json via the existing render path.

truths: Emitted commandWindows contains no dollar no percent no backtick and resolves the git root before requiring the hook
Executing commandWindows from a nested fixture cwd runs the hook successfully on POSIX
prohibitions: Do not change the POSIX command form
Do not introduce a .cmd/.ps1 shim file (single-string transport only, per Codex hook contract)

trace.outcome: commandWindows now a shell-agnostic node -e bootstrap that resolves the real git root before requiring the hook, fixing MODULE_NOT_FOUND from any nested cwd; contract test tightened to a structural regex forbidding $/%/backtick and extended with a real execution test (nested-cwd + non-git-cwd fixture); .codex/hooks.json regenerated via the catalog render path.
trace.verify_output: 180 rows across 19 check group(s) (0 skipped), 0 failing target contract. ALL PASS. Includes new/updated rows: codex-repo-commandWindows-contract (all 13 codex-repo-target entries carry the git-root-resolving node -e SCRIPT bootstrap, no $/%/backtick) and codex-commandWindows-nested-cwd-execution (bootstrap executed from a NESTED fixture cwd resolves the git root and runs the real hook; outside any git repo it exits 0 silently).
